testcase.name: test_request

failure.message: TypeError: '>=' not supported between instances of 'MagicMock' and 'int'

testcase.name: test_request_not_authorized
testcase.failure:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4385686608'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4385693072'> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' spec='Curl' id='4329239120'>
@mock.patch("pycurl.Curl", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_request_not_authorized(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_curl): url = "https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar" auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_curl.side_effect = pycurl.error(400, "NotAuthorizedException")
with pytest.raises( pyschlage.exceptions.NotAuthorizedError, match=f"Unauthorized for url: {url}" ): > auth.request("get", "/foo/bar", baz="bam")
tests/test_auth.py:54: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/auth.py:58: in wrapper resp = fn(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyschlage/auth.py:39: in wrapper return fn(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyschlage/auth.py:126: in request curl = pycurl.Curl() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:1124: in __call__ return self._mock_call(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:1128: in _mock_call return self._execute_mock_call(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='Curl' spec='Curl' id='4329239120'>, args = () kwargs = {}, effect = error(400, 'NotAuthorizedException')
def _execute_mock_call(self, /, *args, **kwargs): # separate from _increment_mock_call so that awaited functions are # executed separately from their call, also AsyncMock overrides this method
effect = self.side_effect if effect is not None: if _is_exception(effect): > raise effect E pycurl.error: (400, 'NotAuthorizedException')
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:1183: error

testcase.name: test_request_unknown_error

failure.message: AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'URL'

testcase.failure:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4385070736'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4385068816'> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' spec='Curl' id='4385067280'>
@mock.patch("pycurl.Curl", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_request_unknown_error(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_curl): url = "https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar" auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_curl_instance = mock_curl.return_value mock_curl_instance.perform.side_effect = pycurl.error(500, "Internal Server Error")
with pytest.raises(pyschlage.exceptions.UnknownError): > auth.request("get", "/foo/bar", baz="bam")
tests/test_auth.py:74: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/auth.py:58: in wrapper resp = fn(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyschlage/auth.py:39: in wrapper return fn(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pyschlage/auth.py:127: in request curl.setopt(curl.URL, f"{base_url}/{path.lstrip('/')}") ^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <NonCallableMagicMock name='Curl()' spec='Curl' id='4384360336'> name = 'URL'
def __getattr__(self, name): if name in {'_mock_methods', '_mock_unsafe'}: raise AttributeError(name) elif self._mock_methods is not None: if name not in self._mock_methods or name in _all_magics: > raise AttributeError("Mock object has no attribute %r" % name) E AttributeError: Mock object has no attribute 'URL'
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:653: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_user_id
testcase.failure:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4385476368'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4385472720'> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4385732176'>
@mock.patch("pycurl.Curl") @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth") @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_user_id(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_curl): auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_curl.return_value = mock.Mock( perform=mock.Mock(), getinfo=mock.Mock(return_value=200), response_body=mock.Mock( json=mock.Mock( return_value={ "consentRecords": [], "created": "2022-12-24T20:00:00.000Z", "email": "asdf@asdf.com", "friendlyName": "username", "identityId": "<user-id>", "lastUpdated": "2022-12-24T20:00:00.000Z", } ) ) ) > assert auth.user_id == "<user-id>" ^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_auth.py:104: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/auth.py:102: in user_id self._user_id = self._get_user_id() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <pyschlage.auth.Auth object at 0x105650510>
def _get_user_id(self) -> str: resp = self.request("GET", "users/@me") > return resp["identityId"] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
pyschlage/auth.py:107: TypeError

testcase.name: test_user_id_is_cached
testcase.failure:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4385105040'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4385110416'> mock_curl = <MagicMock name='Curl' id='4385109264'>
@mock.patch("pycurl.Curl") @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth") @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_user_id_is_cached(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_curl): auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_curl.return_value = mock.Mock( perform=mock.Mock(), getinfo=mock.Mock(return_value=200), response_body=mock.Mock( json=mock.Mock( return_value={ "consentRecords": [], "created": "2022-12-24T20:00:00.000Z", "email": "asdf@asdf.com", "friendlyName": "username", "identityId": "<user-id>", "lastUpdated": "2022-12-24T20:00:00.000Z", } ) ) ) > assert auth.user_id == "<user-id>" ^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_auth.py:133: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/auth.py:102: in user_id self._user_id = self._get_user_id() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <pyschlage.auth.Auth object at 0x1055f5250>
def _get_user_id(self) -> str: resp = self.request("GET", "users/@me") > return resp["identityId"] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
pyschlage/auth.py:107: TypeError

testcase.name: test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule

failure.message:
AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False)
Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show Differing attributes: ['schedule']
Drill down into differing attribute schedule: schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))...
...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_code.TestAccessCode object at 0x105579650> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4385069968'> access_code_json = {'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'accesscodeId': '__access_code_uuid__', 'activationSecs': 0, ...} wifi_device = Device(device_id='__wifi_uuid__', device_type='be489wifi')
def test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule( self, mock_auth: Mock, access_code_json: dict[str, Any], wifi_device: Device ): access_code_id = "__access_code_uuid__" sched = TemporarySchedule( start=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30, 0), end=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0, 0), ) json = deepcopy(access_code_json) json["activationSecs"] = 1671957000 json["expirationSecs"] = 1671958800 code = AccessCode( _auth=mock_auth, _json=json, _device=wifi_device, name="Friendly name", code="0123", schedule=sched, device_id=wifi_device.device_id, access_code_id=access_code_id, ) > assert AccessCode.from_json(mock_auth, wifi_device, json) == code E AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False) E E Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show E Differing attributes: E ['schedule'] E E Drill down into differing attribute schedule: E schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))... E E ...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show
tests/test_code.py:75: AssertionError
